message:
feat: add gt handoff command for agent lifecycle

Implements the agent-side lifecycle request command (gt-gby):

- Detects agent role via GT_ROLE env, tmux session, or directory
- Pre-flight checks (git clean) before retirement
- Sends handoff mail to self for context preservation
- Sends lifecycle request to manager (daemon/ for Mayor/Witness)
- Sets requesting_cycle state and waits for retirement

Usage:
  gt handoff           # Role-appropriate default
  gt handoff --cycle   # Restart with handoff mail
  gt handoff --restart # Fresh restart
  gt handoff --shutdown # Terminate without restart

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"gt-8lz","title":"Comprehensive help text and examples","description":"Improve help text with examples and cross-references.

## Improvements

### 1. Examples Section
Add to Long description:
```go
var spawnCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Long: `Spawn a polecat with work assignment.

Examples:
  gt spawn gastown/Toast --issue gt-abc
  gt spawn gastown --issue gt-def          # auto-select polecat
  gt spawn gastown/Nux -m "Fix the tests"  # free-form task`,
}
```

### 2. Cross-References
Reference related commands:
```
See also:
  gt polecat list    List available polecats
  gt session attach  Attach to spawned session
```

### 3. Flag Descriptions
More detail on flags:
```go
cmd.Flags().StringVar(&issue, "issue", "", 
    "Beads issue ID to assign. The polecat will work on this issue.")
```

### 4. Common Workflows
Add workflow docs to gt --help:
```
Common Workflows:
  Start a swarm:
    gt swarm preflight
    gt swarm create gastown --epic gt-abc --worker Toast --worker Nux --start
    gt refinery start gastown
    
  Check status:
    gt status
    gt swarm status <id>
```

## Files to Update
All internal/cmd/*.go files

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All commands have Examples
- [ ] Related commands cross-referenced
- [ ] Flags have detailed descriptions
- [ ] Root help shows workflows","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:53.303016-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:07:37.391195-08:00"}

-{"id":"gt-99m","title":"gt daemon: Background service management","description":"## Summary

ONE daemon for all Gas Town - a simple Go process (not a Claude agent) that:
1. Pokes agents periodically (heartbeat)
2. Processes lifecycle requests
3. Restarts sessions when agents request cycling

## Architecture

Daemon (gt daemon)
- Pokes Mayor: "HEARTBEAT: check your rigs"
- Pokes each Witness: "HEARTBEAT: check your workers"
- Has inbox at daemon/ for lifecycle requests
- Restarts sessions on cycle requests

## NOT the daemon's job:
- Making decisions (Witnesses do that)
- Running Claude (it's a Go process)
- Processing work (polecats do that)
- Direct polecat management (Witnesses do that)

The daemon is a **dumb scheduler** - poke things, execute lifecycle requests. All intelligence is in agents.

## Commands

- gt daemon start: Start daemon (background)
- gt daemon stop: Stop daemon
- gt daemon status: Show daemon status
- gt daemon logs: View daemon logs

## Daemon Loop

Every heartbeat interval:
1. Poke Mayor
2. For each rig: Poke Witness
3. Process lifecycle requests from daemon/ inbox
4. Check for dead sessions, restart if cycle requested

## Lifecycle Request Handling

Daemon checks its inbox (daemon/) for lifecycle requests:
- From Mayor: cycle/restart Mayor session
- From Witnesses: cycle/restart Witness session

On request:
1. Verify agent state shows requesting_cycle
2. Kill session
3. Start new session
4. Clear requesting_cycle flag

## Poke Protocol

Poke = tmux inject "HEARTBEAT: do your job"
- Agent ignores if already working
- Agent wakes up if idle
- Idempotent - multiple pokes are fine

## Lifecycle Hierarchy

- Daemon manages: Mayor, all Witnesses
- Witness manages: Polecats, Refinery (per rig)
- Crew: self-managed (human workspace)

## Related Issues

- gt-kmn.11: Daemon heartbeat details
- gt-gby: gt handoff command (unified lifecycle)
- gt-u1j.9: Fold witness daemon into this","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:50:09.763719-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T11:49:34.698899-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-99m","depends_on_id":"gt-hw6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:23:43.253877-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-9a2","title":"Federation: Wasteland architecture for cross-town coordination","description":"## Overview

Federation enables Gas Town to scale across machines via **Outposts** - remote compute environments that can run workers.

**Design doc**: docs/federation-design.md

## Outpost Types

| Type | Description | Cost Model |
|------|-------------|------------|
| Local | Current model (tmux panes) | Free |
| SSH/VM | Full Gas Town clone on VM | Always-on |
| CloudRun | Container workers on GCP | Pay-per-use |

## Key Concepts

### Outpost Abstraction
```go
type Outpost interface {
    Name() string
    Type() OutpostType  // local, ssh, cloudrun
    MaxWorkers() int
    Spawn(issue string, config WorkerConfig) (Worker, error)
    Workers() []Worker
    Ping() error
}
```

### Cloud Run Workers
- Persistent HTTP/2 connections solve zero-to-one cold start
- Pay only when working (~$0.017 per 5-min session)
- Scale 0→N automatically
- Git clone via persistent volumes

### SSH/VM Outposts  
- Full Gas Town clone on remote machine
- SSH for commands, git for sync
- Good for long-running autonomous work

## Design Principles

1. **Outpost abstraction** - support multiple backends
2. **Local-first** - remote is for overflow/burst
3. **Git as source of truth** - code and beads sync everywhere
4. **HTTP for Cloud Run** - dont force mail onto containers
5. **Graceful degradation** - works with any subset of outposts

## Related

- gt-f9x.7-10: Connection interface (lower-level abstraction)
- docs/federation-design.md: Full architectural analysis","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:21:32.462063-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T18:01:24.224349-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-9a2.1","title":"Outpost/Worker interfaces: Core abstractions for remote compute","description":"## Overview

Define the core interfaces that all outpost types implement.

## Interfaces

```go
type OutpostType string
const (
    OutpostLocal    OutpostType = "local"
    OutpostSSH      OutpostType = "ssh"
    OutpostCloudRun OutpostType = "cloudrun"
)

type Outpost interface {
    Name() string
    Type() OutpostType
    MaxWorkers() int
    ActiveWorkers() int
    Spawn(issue string, config WorkerConfig) (Worker, error)
    Workers() []Worker
    Ping() error
    SendMail(worker string, msg Message) error  // optional
}

type Worker interface {
    ID() string
    Outpost() string
    Status() WorkerStatus  // idle, working, done, failed
    Issue() string
    Attach() error         // for interactive outposts
    Logs() (io.Reader, error)
    Stop() error
}

type WorkerConfig struct {
    RigPath    string
    BeadsDir   string
    GitBranch  string
    Context    map[string]string  // hints for worker
}
```

## Files

- `internal/outpost/outpost.go` - Outpost interface
- `internal/outpost/worker.go` - Worker interface
- `internal/outpost/config.go` - WorkerConfig, OutpostType

## Notes

This is the foundation for all federation work. Keep interfaces minimal - we can extend later.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:01:38.268086-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T18:01:38.268086-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-9a2.1","depends_on_id":"gt-9a2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:01:38.27131-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-9a2.10","title":"VM outpost setup: Terraform and documentation","description":"## Overview

Documentation and optional Terraform for setting up VM outposts.

## Manual Setup Guide

```markdown
# Setting Up a VM Outpost

## Prerequisites
- GCE VM (or any Linux machine with SSH access)
- SSH key pair
- Git configured on VM

## Steps

1. Install Claude Code on VM:
   ```bash
   ssh user@vm
   npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
   ```

2. Clone Gas Town:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p ~/ai
   cd ~/ai
   gt install .
   ```

3. Configure Git credentials:
   ```bash
   git config --global user.name "Your Name"
   git config --global credential.helper store
   ```

4. Add outpost to local config:
   ```bash
   gt outpost add ssh \
     --name gce-burst \
     --host 10.0.0.5 \
     --user steve \
     --key ~/.ssh/gce_worker \
     --town-path /home/steve/ai \
     --max-workers 8
   ```

5. Test connectivity:
   ```bash
   gt outpost ping gce-burst
   ```
```

## Terraform Module (Optional)

```hcl
# deploy/terraform/vm-outpost/main.tf

variable "project" {}
variable "zone" { default = "us-central1-a" }
variable "machine_type" { default = "e2-standard-4" }

resource "google_compute_instance" "outpost" {
  name         = "gastown-outpost"
  machine_type = var.machine_type
  zone         = var.zone

  boot_disk {
    initialize_params {
      image = "ubuntu-2204-lts"
      size  = 100
    }
  }

  network_interface {
    network = "default"
    access_config {}
  }

  metadata_startup_script = file("${path.module}/startup.sh")
}

output "external_ip" {
  value = google_compute_instance.outpost.network_interface[0].access_config[0].nat_ip
}
```

## Files

- `docs/vm-outpost-setup.md`
- `deploy/terraform/vm-outpost/` (optional)

Depends on: gt-9a2.5 (SSHOutpost)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:03:34.782908-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T18:03:34.782908-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-9a2.10","depends_on_id":"gt-9a2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:03:34.78496-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-9a2.10","depends_on_id":"gt-9a2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:03:46.408609-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-l9g","title":"Beads epic templates for batch work patterns","description":"Optional: Define templates for common batch work patterns.

## Concept

A template encodes a workflow pattern that can be instantiated as beads:

```yaml
# templates/batch-basic.yaml
name: basic-batch
description: Simple batch work pattern
phases:
  - name: startup
    issues:
      - title: "Verify workers ready"
  - name: working
    # Actual work issues added separately
  - name: cleanup
    issues:
      - title: "Merge all branches"
      - title: "Clean up workers"
      - title: "Report to Mayor"
```

## Usage

```bash
gt spawn --template basic-batch --epic gt-u1j --workers 3
```

Creates beads epic with template phases + actual work from gt-u1j children.

## Decision Point

Templates are OPTIONAL. The core design (beads as state, multi-wave orchestration) works without templates. Templates are sugar for common patterns.

Consider deferring to P3 or dropping entirely if beads epics with dependencies suffice.

## Note

No "swarm IDs" involved - templates just pre-populate epic/issue structure.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:51:24.399235-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:26:08.868396-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-lno","title":"Swarm state persistence: manifest + state + events","description":"Upgrade swarm persistence to match PGT pattern.

## Current State
Single .gastown/swarms.json with all swarms.

## Target State (per-swarm)
```
.gastown/swarms/
└── <swarm-id>/
    ├── manifest.json  # Immutable config
    ├── state.json     # Mutable progress
    ├── events.jsonl   # Audit log
    └── report.md      # Generated report
```

## File Schemas

### manifest.json (immutable after creation)
```json
{
  "id": "sw-1",
  "title": "Epic description",
  "epic_id": "gt-abc",
  "rig": "gastown",
  "base_commit": "abc123",
  "integration_branch": "swarm/sw-1",
  "target_branch": "main",
  "workers": ["Toast", "Nux"],
  "tasks": [{"id": "gt-xyz", "title": "..."}],
  "created_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
}
```

### state.json (mutable)
```json
{
  "state": "active",
  "task_states": {
    "gt-xyz": {"state": "merged", "assignee": "Toast"}
  },
  "updated_at": "2024-01-01T01:00:00Z",
  "error": null
}
```

### events.jsonl (append-only audit)
```jsonl
{"event": "created", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
{"event": "task_assigned", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
{"event": "task_merged", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
```

## Benefits
- Audit trail via events.jsonl
- Manifest immutability prevents corruption
- Cleaner separation of concerns
- Per-swarm isolation

## Migration
Keep backward compat with swarms.json during transition.

## Files to Modify
- internal/swarm/manager.go: Refactor persistence
- internal/cmd/swarm.go: SwarmStore → directory-based

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Per-swarm directory structure
- [ ] Events logged to JSONL
- [ ] Manifest immutable after creation
- [ ] List command aggregates from directories","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:14.151538-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:19.716405-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:19.716405-08:00","close_reason":"Swarm state persistence is redundant. Beads IS the persistence layer - epics track batch work, issues track tasks, status tracks progress. No separate manifest/state/events needed. See Key Decision #11 in architecture.md."}
 {"id":"gt-njr","title":"Engineer session restart protocol","description":"Implement session restart flow for when the Engineer needs to split work:

1. Engineer creates subtask(s) in Beads assigned to self
2. Engineer sends handoff mail to self (🤝 HANDOFF)
3. Engineer sends restart request to Witness
4. Witness verifies:
   - Handoff mail exists in Engineer outbox/sent
   - Subtasks filed in Beads
5. Witness restarts the Refinery session (new Engineer context)

This enables "occasionally consistent, eventually convergent" work patterns.
The Refinery continues; the Engineer gets fresh context.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:48.22994-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T23:07:42.05363-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-njr","depends_on_id":"gt-h5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:56.148564-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-oc2","title":"Daemon: proper rig discovery","description":"Currently discovers rigs by scanning tmux session names for gt-*-witness pattern. Should instead:
- Read rigs from mayor/rigs.json
- Or scan town directory for .gastown subdirs
- Handle rigs that exist but don't have running witnesses","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T13:38:15.825299-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T13:38:15.825299-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-oc2","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T13:38:26.826697-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-ouo","title":"gt swarm start: Does not spawn polecat sessions","description":"gt swarm start marks swarm as 'active' but doesn't start any polecat sessions.

Repro:
1. gt swarm create gastown --epic gt-hw6 --worker Toast --worker Nux
2. gt swarm start gt-hw6
3. gt session list - shows no new sessions

Expected: Polecat sessions should start for each worker.
Actual: No sessions started, workers sit idle.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:25:43.430981-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T22:31:58.849531-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T22:31:58.849531-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed: spawn wait time, swarm task discovery accepts 'blocks' deps, swarm start spawns workers"}
 {"id":"gt-ov2","title":"Refinery agent: merge queue processing loop","description":"The Refinery agent processes the merge queue, merging polecat work to main.

## Interface

```go
type Refinery struct {
    rig    *Rig
    queue  *MergeQueue
    git    *Git
    mail   *Mailbox
    config RefineryConfig
}

type RefineryConfig struct {
    AutoMerge     bool          // Auto-merge passing MRs
    RunTests      bool          // Run tests before merge
    TestCommand   string        // Command to run tests
    RequireReview bool          // Require review before merge (future)
}

func NewRefinery(rig *Rig, ...) *Refinery

// Lifecycle
func (r *Refinery) Start() error
func (r *Refinery) Stop() error
func (r *Refinery) IsRunning() bool

// Processing
func (r *Refinery) ProcessQueue() error
func (r *Refinery) ProcessMR(mr *MergeRequest) error
```

## Processing Loop

1. Check queue for pending MRs
2. For each pending MR:
   a. Fetch polecat branch
   b. Attempt merge to refinery/rig (local main)
   c. Run tests if configured
   d. If pass: push to origin, mark merged
   e. If fail: mark rejected, notify polecat
3. Sleep, repeat

## Merge Strategy

- Fast-forward when possible
- Merge commit when not
- On conflict: reject MR, polecat must rebase

## Test Integration

If tests configured:
```bash
cd refinery/rig
git merge polecat/branch
<test_command>  # e.g., go test ./...
```
Result determines merge/reject.

## Notifications

On merge success:
- Mail to polecat: "Your work merged"
- Update bead if issue tracked

On merge failure:
- Mail to polecat: "Merge failed: <reason>"
- Include conflict details if applicable

## Dependencies

 {"id":"gt-u1j.8","title":"Polecat management: add, remove, list, state","description":"Polecat lifecycle: add, remove, list, state tracking.

## Data Model

```go
type Polecat struct {
    Name      string          `json:"name"`
    Rig       string          `json:"rig"`
    State     PolecatState    `json:"state"`
    ClonePath string          `json:"clone_path"`
    Branch    string          `json:"branch"`
    Issue     string          `json:"issue,omitempty"`
    CreatedAt time.Time       `json:"created_at"`
    UpdatedAt time.Time       `json:"updated_at"`
}

type PolecatState string
const (
    StateIdle       PolecatState = "idle"
    StateActive     PolecatState = "active"
    StateWorking    PolecatState = "working"
    StateDone       PolecatState = "done"
    StateStuck      PolecatState = "stuck"
)
```

## Interface

```go
type PolecatManager struct {
    rig *Rig
    git *Git
}

func NewPolecatManager(rig *Rig, git *Git) *PolecatManager

// Lifecycle
func (pm *PolecatManager) Add(name string) (*Polecat, error)
func (pm *PolecatManager) Remove(name string) error
func (pm *PolecatManager) List() ([]*Polecat, error)
func (pm *PolecatManager) Get(name string) (*Polecat, error)

// State
func (pm *PolecatManager) SetState(name string, state PolecatState) error
func (pm *PolecatManager) AssignIssue(name, issue string) error
func (pm *PolecatManager) ClearIssue(name string) error

// Convenience
func (pm *PolecatManager) Wake(name string) error   // idle → active
func (pm *PolecatManager) Sleep(name string) error  // active → idle
```

## Add Flow

1. Create directory: `<rig>/polecats/<name>/`
2. Clone rig repo into it (or copy from rig root)
3. Create new branch: `polecat/<name>`
4. Initialize polecat state file
5. Create mail inbox

## Remove Flow

1. Verify session not running
2. Check for uncommitted changes (warn/error)
3. Remove directory
4. Clean up state

## State Persistence

Store in `<rig>/polecats/<name>/state.json`","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T17:12:27.402824-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T13:37:34.912049-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T13:37:34.912049-08:00","close_reason":"Closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-u1j.8","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-15T17:12:27.403171-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-u1j.8","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T17:13:53.747126-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-u1j.8","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T17:13:53.831197-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-u1j.9","title":"Witness daemon: heartbeat loop, spawn ephemeral agent","description":"Background daemon for agent lifecycle and monitoring.

## Core Responsibilities

1. **Heartbeat monitoring**: Check agent health periodically
2. **Session lifecycle**: Restart agents after handoff (Witness Protection)
3. **Polecat monitoring**: Track worker progress, nudging
4. **Escalation**: Report failures to Mayor

## Session Lifecycle (Witness Protection)

The daemon enables autonomous long-running operation by cycling agent sessions:

```go
type SessionLifecycle struct {
    AgentType string  // "witness", "refinery"
    StatePath string  // path to state.json
}

func (d *Daemon) monitorSessionLifecycle(agent SessionLifecycle) {
    // 1. Detect session exit
    // 2. Check state.json for requesting_cycle: true
    // 3. If cycle requested: start new session, clear flag
    // 4. If unexpected exit: escalate to Mayor
}
```

See architecture.md Key Decision #12: Agent Session Lifecycle.

## Interface

```go
type Daemon struct {
    rig      *Rig
    polecats *PolecatManager
    sessions *SessionManager
    config   DaemonConfig
}

type DaemonConfig struct {
    HeartbeatInterval time.Duration  // default: 30s
    NudgeThreshold    int            // nudges before escalation
    MaxWorkers        int            // from rig config
}

func NewDaemon(rig *Rig) *Daemon
func (d *Daemon) Start() error
func (d *Daemon) Stop() error

// Lifecycle
func (d *Daemon) CycleSession(agentType string) error
func (d *Daemon) SpawnWorker(issueID string) error
func (d *Daemon) ShutdownWorker(polecat string) error
```

## Heartbeat Loop

Every HeartbeatInterval:
1. Check Witness session (restart if cycle requested)
2. Check Refinery session (restart if cycle requested)
3. For each active polecat: assess progress, nudge if stuck
4. Query `bd ready` for new work, spawn up to max_workers","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T17:12:29.389103-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T12:38:32.125316-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T11:50:12.047959-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-u1j.9","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-15T17:12:29.389428-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-u1j.9","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T17:14:04.353775-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-u1j.9","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T17:14:04.440363-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-v5k","title":"Design: Failure modes and recovery","description":"Document failure modes and recovery strategies for Gas Town operations.

## Critical Failure Modes

### 1. Agent Crash Mid-Operation

**Scenario**: Polecat crashes while committing, Witness crashes while verifying

**Detection**:
- Session suddenly gone (tmux check fails)
- State shows 'working' but no session
- Heartbeat stops (for Witness)

**Recovery**:
- Doctor detects via ZombieSessionCheck
- Capture any recoverable state
- Reset agent state to 'idle'
- For Witness: auto-restart via supervisor or manual gt witness start

### 2. Git State Corruption

**Scenario**: Merge conflict, failed rebase, detached HEAD

**Detection**:
- Git commands fail
- Dirty state that won't commit
- Branch diverged from origin

**Recovery**:
- gt doctor reports git health issues
- Manual intervention recommended
- Severe cases: remove clone, re-clone

### 3. Beads Sync Conflict

**Scenario**: Two polecats modify same issue

**Detection**:
- bd sync fails with conflict
- Beads tombstone mechanism handles most cases

**Recovery**:
- Beads has last-write-wins semantics
- bd sync --force in extreme cases
- Issues may need manual dedup

### 4. Tmux Failure

**Scenario**: Tmux server crashes, socket issues

**Detection**:
- All sessions inaccessible
- "no server running" errors

**Recovery**:
- Kill any orphan processes
- tmux kill-server && tmux start-server
- All agent states reset to idle
- Re-spawn active work

### 5. Claude API Issues

**Scenario**: Rate limits, outages, context limits

**Detection**:
- Sessions hang or produce errors
- Repeated failure patterns

**Recovery**:
- Exponential backoff (handled by Claude Code)
- For context limits: session cycling (mail-to-self)
- For outages: wait and retry

### 6. Disk Full

**Scenario**: Clones, logs, or beads fill disk

**Detection**:
- Write operations fail
- git/bd commands error

**Recovery**:
- Clean up logs: rm ~/.gastown/logs/*
- Remove old polecat clones
- gt doctor --fix can clean some cruft

### 7. Network Failure

**Scenario**: Can't reach GitHub, API servers

**Detection**:
- git fetch/push fails
- Claude sessions hang

**Recovery**:
- Work continues locally
- Queue pushes for later
- Sync when connectivity restored

## Recovery Principles

1. **Fail safe**: Prefer stopping over corrupting
2. **State is recoverable**: Git and beads have recovery mechanisms
3. **Doctor heals**: gt doctor --fix handles common issues
4. **Emergency stop**: gt stop --all as last resort
5. **Human escalation**: Some failures need Overseer intervention

## Implementation

- Document each failure mode in architecture.md
- Ensure doctor checks cover detection
- Add recovery hints to error messages
- Log all failures for debugging","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:07.198289-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:28.171942-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-vci","title":"Mayor handoff mail template","description":"Add MAYOR_HANDOFF mail template to templates.py.

## Template Function

def mayor_handoff(
    active_swarms: List[SwarmStatus],
    rig_status: Dict[str, RigStatus],
    pending_escalations: List[Escalation],
    in_flight_decisions: List[Decision],
    recent_actions: List[str],
    delegated_work: List[DelegatedItem],
    user_requests: List[str],
    next_steps: List[str],
    warnings: Optional[str] = None,
    session_duration: Optional[str] = None,
) -> Message:
    metadata = {
        'template': 'MAYOR_HANDOFF',
        'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
        'session_duration': session_duration,
        'active_swarm_count': len(active_swarms),
        'pending_escalation_count': len(pending_escalations),
    }
    # ... format sections ...
    return Message.create(
        sender='mayor/',
        recipient='mayor/',
        subject='Session Handoff',
        body=body,
        priority='high',
    )

## Metadata Fields

- template: MAYOR_HANDOFF
- timestamp: ISO format
- session_duration: Human readable
- active_swarm_count: Number of active swarms
- pending_escalation_count: Number of escalations

## Mail Priority

Use priority='high' to ensure handoff is seen on startup.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:30.26323-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:59.550689-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-vci","depends_on_id":"gt-u82","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:39.554108-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-vjw","title":"Swarm learning: Session cleanup missing from swarm workflow","description":"## Problem

After Enders Game swarm completed (18 issues merged), 16 polecat sessions were left running but idle. No automated cleanup occurred.

## What Should Happen

1. Witness detects polecat completed work (idle at prompt)
2. Witness verifies git state is clean
3. Witness shuts down session
4. Witness reports completion to Mayor

## GGT Components

- gt-cxx: Witness context cycling (covers self-cycling)
- gt-u1j.9: Witness daemon heartbeat loop
- gt-kmn.6: Witness swarm landing protocol

## Recommendation

Add to Witness responsibilities:
- Monitor for 'work complete' signals (DONE keyword, idle detection)
- Automated session shutdown after verification
- Swarm completion reporting to Mayor

 {"id":"gt-z4g","title":"Plugin: Plan-to-Epic converter","description":"## Purpose

Help users create beads epics from various planning inputs.

## Inputs
- Markdown task lists
- GitHub issues
- Linear/Jira exports
- Free-form descriptions
- Existing beads epics

## Output
- Beads epic with properly structured children
- Dependencies set for wave ordering
- Priorities assigned
- Ready for `gt spawn --epic <id>`

## Implementation Options

### Option A: CLI Tool
```bash
gt plan import --from github --repo owner/repo --label batch-candidate
gt plan import --from markdown tasks.md
gt plan structure <epic-id>  # analyze and add dependencies
```

### Option B: Plugin Agent
A plugin at `<rig>/plugins/plan-oracle/` that:
- Receives planning requests via mail
- Analyzes scope and requirements
- Creates structured beads epic
- Sets dependencies based on analysis

### Option C: Interactive Mode
```bash
gt plan create
# Walks through questions, creates epic interactively
```

## Axiom

As stated: 'The Planning phase should end in the creation of a workable Beads plan.'

This plugin bridges the gap between human planning and machine-executable work.

## Priority

P2 - Nice to have for MVP. Manual epic creation works for now.

## Note

No "swarm IDs" - output is just a beads epic with children. Workers process it independently.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T02:10:20.663549-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:26:41.087304-08:00"}

- Witness status

- Beads summary (open issues count)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:47:17.879255-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T22:29:46.970197-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T22:29:46.970197-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented gt rig info command with detailed rig status display","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-zko","depends_on_id":"gt-hw6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:22:47.502099-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,351 @@
+package cmd
+
+import (
+	"encoding/json"
+	"fmt"
+	"os"
+	"os/exec"
+	"path/filepath"
+	"strings"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
+	"github.com/steveyegge/gastown/internal/style"
+	"github.com/steveyegge/gastown/internal/workspace"
+)
+
+// HandoffAction for handoff command.
+type HandoffAction string
+
+const (
+	HandoffCycle    HandoffAction = "cycle"    // Restart with handoff mail
+	HandoffRestart  HandoffAction = "restart"  // Fresh restart, no handoff
+	HandoffShutdown HandoffAction = "shutdown" // Terminate, no restart
+)
+
+var handoffCmd = &cobra.Command{
+	Use:   "handoff",
+	Short: "Request lifecycle action (retirement/restart)",
+	Long: `Request a lifecycle action from your manager.
+
+This command initiates graceful retirement:
+1. Verifies git state is clean
+2. Sends handoff mail to yourself (for cycle)
+3. Sends lifecycle request to your manager
+4. Sets requesting state and waits for retirement
+
+Your manager (daemon for Mayor/Witness, witness for polecats) will
+verify the request and terminate your session. For cycle/restart,
+a new session starts and reads your handoff mail to continue work.
+
+Flags:
+  --cycle     Restart with handoff mail (default for Mayor/Witness)
+  --restart   Fresh restart, no handoff context
+  --shutdown  Terminate without restart (default for polecats)
+
+Examples:
+  gt handoff           # Use role-appropriate default
+  gt handoff --cycle   # Restart with context handoff
+  gt handoff --restart # Fresh restart
+`,
+	RunE: runHandoff,
+}
+
+var (
+	handoffCycle    bool
+	handoffRestart  bool
+	handoffShutdown bool
+	handoffForce    bool
+	handoffMessage  string
+)
+
+func init() {
+	handoffCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&handoffCycle, "cycle", false, "Restart with handoff mail")
+	handoffCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&handoffRestart, "restart", false, "Fresh restart, no handoff")
+	handoffCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&handoffShutdown, "shutdown", false, "Terminate without restart")
+	handoffCmd.Flags().BoolVarP(&handoffForce, "force", "f", false, "Skip pre-flight checks")
+	handoffCmd.Flags().StringVarP(&handoffMessage, "message", "m", "", "Handoff message for successor")
+
+	rootCmd.AddCommand(handoffCmd)
+}
+
+func runHandoff(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
+	// Detect our role
+	role := detectHandoffRole()
+	if role == RoleUnknown {
+		return fmt.Errorf("cannot detect agent role (set GT_ROLE or run from known context)")
+	}
+
+	// Determine action
+	action := determineAction(role)
+
+	fmt.Printf("Agent role: %s\n", style.Bold.Render(string(role)))
+	fmt.Printf("Action: %s\n", style.Bold.Render(string(action)))
+
+	// Find workspace
+	townRoot, err := workspace.FindFromCwdOrError()
+	if err != nil {
+		return fmt.Errorf("not in a Gas Town workspace: %w", err)
+	}
+
+	// Pre-flight checks (unless forced)
+	if !handoffForce {
+		if err := preFlightChecks(); err != nil {
+			return fmt.Errorf("pre-flight check failed: %w\n\nUse --force to skip checks", err)
+		}
+	}
+
+	// For cycle, send handoff mail to self
+	if action == HandoffCycle {
+		if err := sendHandoffMail(role, townRoot); err != nil {
+			return fmt.Errorf("sending handoff mail: %w", err)
+		}
+		fmt.Printf("%s Sent handoff mail to self\n", style.Bold.Render("✓"))
+	}
+
+	// Send lifecycle request to manager
+	manager := getManager(role)
+	if err := sendLifecycleRequest(manager, role, action, townRoot); err != nil {
+		return fmt.Errorf("sending lifecycle request: %w", err)
+	}
+	fmt.Printf("%s Sent %s request to %s\n", style.Bold.Render("✓"), action, manager)
+
+	// Set requesting state
+	if err := setRequestingState(role, action, townRoot); err != nil {
+		fmt.Printf("Warning: failed to set state: %v\n", err)
+	}
+
+	// Wait for retirement
+	fmt.Println()
+	fmt.Printf("%s Waiting for retirement...\n", style.Dim.Render("◌"))
+	fmt.Println(style.Dim.Render("(Manager will terminate this session)"))
+
+	// Block forever - manager will kill us
+	select {}
+}
+
+// detectHandoffRole figures out what kind of agent we are.
+// Uses GT_ROLE env var, tmux session name, or directory context.
+func detectHandoffRole() Role {
+	// Check GT_ROLE environment variable first
+	if role := os.Getenv("GT_ROLE"); role != "" {
+		switch strings.ToLower(role) {
+		case "mayor":
+			return RoleMayor
+		case "witness":
+			return RoleWitness
+		case "refinery":
+			return RoleRefinery
+		case "polecat":
+			return RolePolecat
+		case "crew":
+			return RoleCrew
+		}
+	}
+
+	// Check tmux session name
+	out, err := exec.Command("tmux", "display-message", "-p", "#{session_name}").Output()
+	if err == nil {
+		sessionName := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
+		if sessionName == "gt-mayor" {
+			return RoleMayor
+		}
+		if strings.HasSuffix(sessionName, "-witness") {
+			return RoleWitness
+		}
+		if strings.HasSuffix(sessionName, "-refinery") {
+			return RoleRefinery
+		}
+		// Polecat sessions: gt-<rig>-<name>
+		if strings.HasPrefix(sessionName, "gt-") && strings.Count(sessionName, "-") >= 2 {
+			return RolePolecat
+		}
+	}
+
+	// Fall back to directory-based detection
+	cwd, err := os.Getwd()
+	if err != nil {
+		return RoleUnknown
+	}
+
+	townRoot, err := workspace.FindFromCwd()
+	if err != nil || townRoot == "" {
+		return RoleUnknown
+	}
+
+	ctx := detectRole(cwd, townRoot)
+	return ctx.Role
+}
+
+// determineAction picks the action based on flags or role default.
+func determineAction(role Role) HandoffAction {
+	// Explicit flags take precedence
+	if handoffCycle {
+		return HandoffCycle
+	}
+	if handoffRestart {
+		return HandoffRestart
+	}
+	if handoffShutdown {
+		return HandoffShutdown
+	}
+
+	// Role-based defaults
+	switch role {
+	case RolePolecat:
+		return HandoffShutdown // Ephemeral, work is done
+	case RoleMayor, RoleWitness, RoleRefinery:
+		return HandoffCycle // Long-running, preserve context
+	case RoleCrew:
+		return HandoffCycle // Will only send mail, not actually retire
+	default:
+		return HandoffCycle
+	}
+}
+
+// preFlightChecks verifies it's safe to retire.
+func preFlightChecks() error {
+	// Check git status
+	cmd := exec.Command("git", "status", "--porcelain")
+	out, err := cmd.Output()
+	if err != nil {
+		// Not a git repo, that's fine
+		return nil
+	}
+
+	if len(strings.TrimSpace(string(out))) > 0 {
+		return fmt.Errorf("uncommitted changes in git working tree")
+	}
+
+	return nil
+}
+
+// getManager returns the address of our lifecycle manager.
+func getManager(role Role) string {
+	switch role {
+	case RoleMayor, RoleWitness:
+		return "daemon/"
+	case RolePolecat, RoleRefinery:
+		// Would need rig context to determine witness address
+		// For now, use a placeholder pattern
+		return "<rig>/witness"
+	case RoleCrew:
+		return "human" // Crew is human-managed
+	default:
+		return "daemon/"
+	}
+}
+
+// sendHandoffMail sends a handoff message to ourselves for the successor to read.
+func sendHandoffMail(role Role, townRoot string) error {
+	// Determine our address
+	var selfAddr string
+	switch role {
+	case RoleMayor:
+		selfAddr = "mayor/"
+	case RoleWitness:
+		selfAddr = "witness/" // Would need rig prefix
+	default:
+		selfAddr = string(role) + "/"
+	}
+
+	// Build handoff message
+	subject := "🤝 HANDOFF: Session cycling"
+	body := handoffMessage
+	if body == "" {
+		body = fmt.Sprintf(`Handoff from previous session.
+
+Time: %s
+Role: %s
+Action: cycle
+
+Check bd ready for pending work.
+Check gt mail inbox for messages received during transition.
+`, time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339), role)
+	}
+
+	// Send via bd mail
+	cmd := exec.Command("bd", "mail", "send",
+		"--to", selfAddr,
+		"--subject", subject,
+		"--body", body,
+	)
+	cmd.Dir = townRoot
+
+	if out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
+		return fmt.Errorf("%w: %s", err, string(out))
+	}
+
+	return nil
+}
+
+// sendLifecycleRequest sends the lifecycle request to our manager.
+func sendLifecycleRequest(manager string, role Role, action HandoffAction, townRoot string) error {
+	if manager == "human" {
+		// Crew is human-managed, just print a message
+		fmt.Println(style.Dim.Render("(Crew sessions are human-managed, no lifecycle request sent)"))
+		return nil
+	}
+
+	subject := fmt.Sprintf("LIFECYCLE: %s requesting %s", role, action)
+	body := fmt.Sprintf(`Lifecycle request from %s.
+
+Action: %s
+Time: %s
+
+Please verify state and execute lifecycle action.
+`, role, action, time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339))
+
+	// Send via bd mail
+	cmd := exec.Command("bd", "mail", "send",
+		"--to", manager,
+		"--subject", subject,
+		"--body", body,
+	)
+	cmd.Dir = townRoot
+
+	if out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
+		return fmt.Errorf("%w: %s", err, string(out))
+	}
+
+	return nil
+}
+
+// setRequestingState updates state.json to indicate we're requesting lifecycle action.
+func setRequestingState(role Role, action HandoffAction, townRoot string) error {
+	// Determine state file location based on role
+	var stateFile string
+	switch role {
+	case RoleMayor:
+		stateFile = filepath.Join(townRoot, "mayor", "state.json")
+	case RoleWitness:
+		// Would need rig context
+		stateFile = filepath.Join(townRoot, "witness", "state.json")
+	default:
+		// For other roles, use a generic location
+		stateFile = filepath.Join(townRoot, ".gastown", "agent-state.json")
+	}
+
+	// Ensure directory exists
+	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(stateFile), 0755); err != nil {
+		return err
+	}
+
+	// Read existing state or create new
+	state := make(map[string]interface{})
+	if data, err := os.ReadFile(stateFile); err == nil {
+		json.Unmarshal(data, &state)
+	}
+
+	// Set requesting state
+	state["requesting_"+string(action)] = true
+	state["requesting_time"] = time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339)
+
+	// Write back
+	data, err := json.MarshalIndent(state, "", "  ")
+	if err != nil {
+		return err
+	}
+
+	return os.WriteFile(stateFile, data, 0644)
+}
